Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Fun with CSF firmware (RK3588 GPU firmware) 53 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Algorithm Visualizer 167 upvotes, 8 comments
  3. Show HN: DuckDB community extension for prefiltered HNSW using ACORN-1 81 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Microbenchmarking Chipsets for Giggles 37 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. Show HN: I took back Video.js after 16 years and we rewrote it to be 88% smaller 522 upvotes, 102 comments
  6. Ubuntu wants to strip some of GRUB features in 26.10 for security purposes 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. VNDB founder Yorhel has died 119 upvotes, 23 comments
  8. Show HN: Email.md – Markdown to responsive, email-safe HTML 347 upvotes, 85 comments
  9. TurboQuant: Redefining AI efficiency with extreme compression 348 upvotes, 97 comments
  10. Antimatter has been transported for the first time 54 upvotes, 11 comments
  11. The Last Testaments of Richard II and Henry IV 57 upvotes, 12 comments
  12. Wine 11 rewrites how Linux runs Windows games at kernel with massive speed gains 1143 upvotes, 408 comments
  13. Flighty Airports 436 upvotes, 154 comments
  14. My Astrophotography in the Movie Project Hail Mary 225 upvotes, 85 comments
  15. Local LLM App by Ente 177 upvotes, 66 comments
  16. I wanted to build vertical SaaS for pest control, so I took a technician job 373 upvotes, 154 comments
  17. Tell HN: Litellm 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 on PyPI are compromised 829 upvotes, 458 comments
  18. Apple Business 685 upvotes, 382 comments
  19. Meta told to pay $375M for misleading users over child safety 376 upvotes, 201 comments
  20. Supreme Court Sides with Cox in Copyright Fight over Pirated Music 70 upvotes, 30 comments
  21. VitruvianOS – Desktop Linux Inspired by the BeOS 262 upvotes, 157 comments
  22. Building a coding agent in Swift from scratch 27 upvotes, 10 comments
  23. Goodbye to Sora 933 upvotes, 682 comments
  24. Arm AGI CPU 385 upvotes, 283 comments
  25. You can run a DNS server (2025) 120 upvotes, 79 comments
  26. Why I forked httpx 196 upvotes, 141 comments
  27. Apple Just Lost Me 316 upvotes, 295 comments
  28. Looking at Unity made me understand the point of C++ coroutines 93 upvotes, 80 comments
  29. In Edison’s Revenge, Data Centers Are Transitioning From AC to DC 218 upvotes, 261 comments
  30. Miscellanea: The War in Iran 140 upvotes, 167 comments

Last generated 12:00am, 26 March 2026. Source here.
